title: Emotional Intelligence: Why It Can Matter More Than IQ
subtitle: The groundbreaking book that launched the EQ revolution — arguing that self-awareness, empathy, impulse control, and social skill matter more than raw intellect for success in life.
author: Daniel Goleman
themes: The two-mind model: rational vs emotional brain
Emotional hijacking and the amygdala's role
Self-awareness as the keystone of emotional competence
Empathy as a fundamental social skill
The plasticity of emotional circuits through neuroplasticity
The cost of emotional illiteracy across relationships, health, and work
awards: New York Times Best Seller (1.5 years on list)
TIME Magazine 25 Most Influential Business Management Books
excerpt: IQ is not enough. Self-awareness, impulse control, empathy, and social deftness — emotional intelligence — matter more for success in relationships, work, and health than the analytical skills measured by IQ tests. And these skills can be taught.
